content:
# Accessibility Walk-Through Checklist

**Tool ID:** T037-01
**Block:** B037 (Accessibility & Inclusivity Standards)
**Domain:** Customer Experience & Service (D02_customer_experience)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Owner
**Time Required:** 15-20 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. Every customer deserves to feel welcome. This checklist helps you see your cafe through the eyes of someone who experiences it differently.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Owner working in Customer Experience & Service.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Structured checklist covering physical access (entrance, pathways, counter, seating, toilets), communication (menu formats, staff interaction), sensory environment, and digital presence. Used for initial audit and annual reviews.

This tool supports **Accessibility & Inclusivity Standards** (B037) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Owner (ideally with input from a customer or team member with lived experience of disability)
**Time Required:** 15-20 minutes
**Frequency:** Full audit annually. Quick walkthrough quarterly. After any physical changes to the venue.

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (printed)
- Tape measure (for doorway widths, table heights)
- Your cafe menu in all available formats
- Your website/social media on a phone

**Steps:**
1. Start outside the front door -- approach as a customer would
2. Work through each section physically walking the space
3. Try to navigate in a wheelchair or with eyes closed for 30 seconds to build empathy
4. Mark each item: PASS / PARTIAL / FAIL / N/A
5. For PARTIAL or FAIL, note the specific barrier and estimated cost/effort to fix
6. Prioritise: safety issues first, then high-impact / low-cost improvements

---

## Checklist

### Section 1: Approach and Entrance

| # | Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | N/A | Notes / Barrier Identified |
|---|------|------|---------|------|-----|---------------------------|
| 1.1 | Entrance visible and clearly marked from the street | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.2 | Step-free access to the entrance (ramp or level entry) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.3 | Door width minimum 80 cm (measure: _____ cm) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.4 | Door can be opened with one hand / automatic / propped open | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.5 | Door handle at accessible height (90-110 cm from floor) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.6 | External signage readable (contrast, font size, lighting) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.7 | A-board or outdoor furniture does not block pavement access | P | Pa | F | | |

### Section 2: Interior Navigation

| # | Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | N/A | Notes / Barrier Identified |
|---|------|------|---------|------|-----|---------------------------|
| 2.1 | Clear pathway from entrance to counter (min 90 cm wide) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.2 | No trip hazards (loose cables, uneven flooring, mats) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.3 | Counter has a lower section or staff can come to the customer | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.4 | At least 2 tables accessible for wheelchair users (clear floor space, no fixed stools) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.5 | Table height suitable (70-80 cm, knee clearance underneath) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.6 | Adequate lighting -- no very dark corners in seating area | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.7 | Floor surface non-slip, even when wet | P | Pa | F | | |

### Section 3: Toilets

| # | Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | N/A | Notes / Barrier Identified |
|---|------|------|---------|------|-----|---------------------------|
| 3.1 | Accessible toilet available (or clear signage to nearest one) | P | Pa | F | N/A | |
| 3.2 | Accessible toilet door opens outward or slides (min 80 cm wide) | P | Pa | F | N/A | |
| 3.3 | Grab rails installed correctly | P | Pa | F | N/A | |
| 3.4 | Accessible toilet not used as storage room | P | Pa | F | N/A | |
| 3.5 | Emergency cord hangs to floor level (not tied up) | P | Pa | F | N/A | |
| 3.6 | Baby changing facilities available | P | Pa | F | N/A | |

### Section 4: Communication and Menu

| # | Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | N/A | Notes / Barrier Identified |
|---|------|------|---------|------|-----|---------------------------|
| 4.1 | Menu available in large print (min 14pt font) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.2 | Menu board readable from a seated position | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.3 | Allergen information clearly available (not just "ask staff") | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.4 | Staff trained to assist customers who need help ordering | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.5 | Staff speak clearly and make eye contact (not just gestures) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.6 | Payment terminal can be brought to the customer | P | Pa | F | | |

### Section 5: Sensory Environment

| # | Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | N/A | Notes / Barrier Identified |
|---|------|------|---------|------|-----|---------------------------|
| 5.1 | Background music volume allows conversation | P | Pa | F | | |
| 5.2 | Grinder noise managed (not directly next to seating) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 5.3 | Quiet seating area available (away from machine noise) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 5.4 | Lighting not excessively bright or flickering | P | Pa | F | | |
| 5.5 | No strong competing smells beyond coffee aroma | P | Pa | F | | |

### Section 6: Digital Presence

| # | Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | N/A | Notes / Barrier Identified |
|---|------|------|---------|------|-----|---------------------------|
| 6.1 | Website/social media mentions accessibility features | P | Pa | F | | |
| 6.2 | Online menu readable on mobile (not just a PDF image) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 6.3 | Contact method available for accessibility queries before visiting | P | Pa | F | | |
| 6.4 | Google Maps listing shows wheelchair accessibility status | P | Pa | F | | |

### Summary Score

| Section | Items | Pass | Partial | Fail | N/A |
|---------|-------|------|---------|------|-----|
| 1. Entrance | 7 | | | | |
| 2. Interior | 7 | | | | |
| 3. Toilets | 6 | | | | |
| 4. Communication | 6 | | | | |
| 5. Sensory | 5 | | | | |
| 6. Digital | 4 | | | | |
| **TOTAL** | **35** | | | | |

**Completion:**
- Date: _______________
- Completed by: _______________
- Co-audited with (if applicable): _______________
- Top 3 improvements to make: 1. _____ 2. _____ 3. _____
- Next review date: _______________

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Accessible Toilet Used as Storage

**Why it happens:** Small venue, limited space. Staff store mops, boxes, or supplies in the accessible toilet because "nobody uses it." Then someone needs it.
**How to prevent:** Clear policy: accessible toilet is never used for storage. Find alternative storage. Check weekly.
**If it is already happening:** Clear it today. Find another home for those items. Put a small sign inside: "This toilet must remain clear and accessible at all times."

### Risk 2: A-Board Blocks Pavement Access

**Why it happens:** Staff place the A-board where it gets most visibility, which is often right in the middle of the pavement. Wheelchair users, pram users, and visually impaired people cannot pass.
**How to prevent:** Mark the A-board position with tape on the ground. Ensure minimum 1.5 m clear pavement width. Check local council regulations.
**If it is already happening:** Move the A-board to sit flat against your building frontage. Measure the remaining pavement width.

### Risk 3: Staff Unsure How to Help

**Why it happens:** Team members want to be helpful but feel awkward or afraid of saying the wrong thing. So they either hover or avoid the customer entirely.
**How to prevent:** Simple training: "Ask, do not assume. Offer help, do not insist. Speak to the person, not their companion." Role-play in a team meeting.
**If it is already happening:** Brief the team at the next shift: "If a customer needs help, ask: Can I help you with anything? Then follow their lead."

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Focus on what you can control. Even small venues can improve communication, menu formats, and staff awareness.
**Chain / Franchise:** Align with brand accessibility standards. Use this as a gap analysis against corporate requirements.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Focus on Sections 1, 4, and 5. Counter height and menu readability are your key areas.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add display case visibility (can items be seen from a seated position?) and assisted service for selecting items.

**Making it yours:**
- Invite a customer with accessibility needs to co-audit -- their perspective is invaluable
- Prioritise low-cost, high-impact changes first (menu formats, staff training, A-board position)
- Display an "All Welcome" message or accessibility features summary near the entrance
- Review after any renovation or furniture change

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T037-02 -- Barrier Improvement Tracker (L1, tracker)
- T037-03 -- Disability Awareness Training Guide (L2, SOP)
- T037-04 -- Accessibility Feedback Form (L2, template)
- T037-05 -- Annual Accessibility Audit Report (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**
- B036

**Parent block:** B037 -- Accessibility & Inclusivity Standards
**Module:** M02: Customer Journey & Retention
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
